definition: Copper supply constraints (7-12 year mine development timeline, 1-2% supply growth vs 3-5% electrification demand growth) create a projected deficit for 2026-2028 that could serve as a structural commodity vol floor. The timing coincidence with the credit maturity wall is potentially significant but involves a long causal chain.
evidence: 7-12 year mine development timeline is a physical constraint
1-2% supply growth vs 3-5% electrification demand growth
Timing coincidence with credit maturity wall
Vol transmission mechanism involves long causal chain: commodity scarcity to capex uncertainty to inflation to rates vol
open_questions: How many links in the causal chain (commodity scarcity to vol) introduce meaningful uncertainty?
Is the maturity wall timing coincidence meaningful or spurious?
Can copper substitution or recycling offset the supply deficit?
